transcript: The movie camera lens. These precision optics can cost up to $50,000 a piece. While digital technology has replaced film cameras, lenses are now a perfect hybrid of old and new technology to focus accurately and control light for each shot. On top of the inner barrel, a low-friction tough polymer stud fits into a spiral channel on the inside of the focus ring. Just like on a regular camera, as this turns, the inner barrel slides forwards and backwards. This moves the glass discs inside to give a razor-sharp focus. Inside a metal ring sit eight overlapping stainless steel leaves just two-thousandths of an inch thick. Miniature pivots link them together to form a circular pattern. As the ring turns, it opens and closes the leaf formation, changing the size of the hole at the center. Just one smooth motion controls the entire ring. This is the first time I've ever seen such a thing. The exact amount of light entering the camera. The lens's Oscar-winning look is down to modern technology and old-fashioned craftsmanship working in harmony.
